frameworks.name: predictive coding
frameworks.one_line: Biologically plausible alternative to backpropagation for training feedforward networks.

frameworks.name: backpropagation
frameworks.one_line: Standard but biologically unrealistic gradient-based training algorithm.

frameworks.name: artificial neural networks
frameworks.one_line: Feedforward supervised learning models interpreted as abstract biological networks.

core_question: What is the mathematical relationship between predictive coding and backpropagation when training feedforward artificial neural networks on supervised tasks?
key_assumptions: ANNs serve as abstract models of biological neuronal networks
Supervised learning on feedforward architectures is the relevant setting
manipulations: Training algorithm: predictive coding vs backpropagation
key_findings: Reviews and extends recent results formalizing the mathematical correspondence between predictive coding and backpropagation
interpretations: Findings carry implications for interpreting predictive coding as a biologically plausible training mechanism
Findings inform interpretation of deep learning models as accounts of neural information processing
